overview: Kurume, located in Fukuoka Prefecture on the island of Kyushu, Japan, is a vibrant city known for its rich cultural heritage and significant role in regional commerce. With a population of approximately 303,579, it serves as a vital hub for both industry and agriculture. Kurume is particularly famous for its textiles and ceramics, attracting visitors and businesses alike, making it an essential part of Japan's economic landscape.
history: Kurume was officially established in the early Edo period and has historically been an important center for the textile industry. Its strategic location along the Chikugo River facilitated trade and cultural exchange.
economy: The economy of Kurume is diverse, with key industries including textiles, ceramics, and agriculture. The city is known for its production of high-quality fabrics and traditional crafts, playing a crucial role in both local and national markets.
tourism: Tourists in Kurume can explore a variety of attractions, including the historic Kurume Castle and the beautiful Tachiarai Peace Memorial Museum. The city is also famous for its hot springs and local festivals, offering visitors a glimpse into traditional Japanese culture and hospitality.

best_restaurants.name: Yakiniku Shichirin
best_restaurants.description: A popular spot for delicious grilled meats and local specialties.

best_restaurants.name: Kurume Ramen Shokudo
best_restaurants.description: Famous for its rich tonkotsu ramen, a must-try when in the city.

best_restaurants.name: Sushi Shun
best_restaurants.description: Offers a variety of fresh sushi made from locally sourced ingredients.

best_restaurants.name: Kushida Soba
best_restaurants.description: Known for its traditional soba noodles and warm atmosphere.

best_restaurants.name: Cafe de Kurume
best_restaurants.description: A cozy cafe perfect for enjoying coffee and local desserts.

weather_summary: Kurume experiences mild winters and hot, humid summers, with a fair amount of rainfall throughout the year.

faqs.question: What time zone is Kurume in?
faqs.answer: Kurume is in the Asia/Tokyo time zone.

faqs.question: Does Kurume observe daylight saving time?
faqs.answer: No, Kurume does not observe daylight saving time.

faqs.question: What is the population of Kurume?
faqs.answer: The population of Kurume is approximately 303,579.

faqs.question: When does sunrise typically occur in Kurume?
faqs.answer: Sunrise in Kurume typically occurs between 5:30 AM and 6:00 AM, depending on the season.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Kurume?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Kurume is during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when the weather is pleasant.
